denial_pattern_sources: peer_to_peer_window_verbal_only: AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Physician Survey; documented in multiple state PA reform laws (TX HB 2002, VA SB 1272)
ignored_physician_letter: AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Physician Survey
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

This letter advises you of our determination regarding your provider's request on 09/13/2026 for Apalutamide plus androgen deprivation therapy related to Metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (C61). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. A less costly modality is appropriate before Apalutamide plus androgen deprivation therapy for Metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (C61). Plan policy requires step-down diagnostic or therapeutic approach.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with modality substitution. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the verbal timeframe communicated to office staff (not confirmed in this letter).

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management

Standard criteria apply regardless of information previously sent to the plan.
clinical_context: The 67-year-old member has Metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (C61). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Apalutamide plus androgen deprivation therapy recommended as the next appropriate step. Rising PSA 18 ng/mL on ADT alone; bone scan negative. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that modality substitution bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
